branch_metadata.branch_rationale: Gravity / black-hole observable; matter-side per Einstein-eq source coupling

description: Kerr-defect geometry of the rotating relational defect. The Kerr line element is reconstructed from the angular momentum J extracted via causal-asymmetry of the rotating defect lattice modes; the dimensionless spin chi = J/M^2 (in lattice-mass units M_lu == 1) is the directly observable quantity. The auxiliary field a_kerr_lu records the Kerr length parameter under a separate Kerr-coordinate normalization used for the metric line element. The inner/outer horizons r_minus/r_plus, the equatorial ergosphere r_ergo_equator, the horizon angular velocity Omega_horizon, the innermost stable circular orbit r_ISCO, and the Lense-Thirring frame-dragging angular velocity Omega_LT are derived in closed form from the Kerr metric.
units_convention: chi_spin = J_lu / M_lu^2 with M_lu == 1 (so chi numerically equals J_lu in this normalization). a_kerr_lu uses the metric-line-element convention; the two fields are consistent up to the M-normalization choice and are not equal as numbers.
kerr_metric: ds^2 = -(1 - r_S r/Sigma) dt^2 - (2 r_S r a sin^2(theta)/Sigma) dt dphi + (Sigma/Delta) dr^2 + Sigma dtheta^2 + [(r^2 + a^2) sin^2(theta) + r_S r a^2 sin^4(theta)/Sigma] dphi^2
derivation: Angular momentum J from CVI-based causal asymmetry of the rotating defect lattice modes; Kerr parameter a = J/M.
framing: The Kerr horizon and ISCO are determined by (M, a) on the lattice; no Kerr ansatz is imposed externally. The two regimes give independent Kerr structures with sub-extremal spin (chi < 0.05 in both).
